Petition For Authority To Invest Funds
Off Calendar Aug-09-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Melanie Emmons, via ZOOM, for David Levin, Trustee.

The court begins by advising counsel that it is ready to take the petition under submission but would like a few clarifications prior to doing so. The court then confirms with counsel that there is a resignation from Gene Marchi as Trustee and acceptance from Mr. Levin as Successor Trustee. The court and counsel then discuss if Trustee will be requesting fees for managing the estate if the investment advisor at the Morgan Stanley firm will be paid up to one (1) percent to manage the portfolio. Counsel advises that Mr. Levin will continue to be paid at an hourly rate.

After additional discussion, the Petition to Invest Funds is submitted.

Judge: Hon. Mary Wiss. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: B. Tso. Not reported.

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (1971 22nd Ave, San Francisco Ca 94116)
Off Calendar Jan-26-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Melanie Emmons appeared by zoom for and with David Levin (Trustee) by zoom.
Tim Hawko (Broker for the listing Agent) appeared by zoom.
Jennifer Rosdail (Agent for the Buyers) appeared by zoom.
Kathy Lam and Alex Doubov (Buyers) appeared by zoom.

No overbidders were present. The Court noted that it received the re-appraisal for sale and that the price is consistent with the re-appraisal value of the property. The sale was confirmed at $1,415,000.00 based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property. An additional bond in the amount of $827,000.00 is required.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

Petition For Authority To Borrow Funds For A Reverse Mortgage Loan
Off Calendar Jun-23-2020 Granted Per Written Order

Petition For Authority To Borrow Funds For A Reverse Mortgage Loan
Off Calendar Jun-03-2020 Continued To Jun-23-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Melanie Emmons and Brent Kato for and with David Levin. All parties appeared telephonically.

Counsel addressed the issues raised by the Court with respect to Mr. Lopresti's capacity to disclaim his interest in the property, whether Probate Code 285(b)(3) precluded Mr. Lopresti from disclaiming his interest in the property based on his continuous occupancy of the property, whether it was intended to be a qualified disclaimer under Internal Revenue Code 2518, whether Mr. Lopresti disclaimed his future interest in receiving distributions from the Child's Trust, the validility of the disclaimer, etc. Discussion was held as to the SPCA's position, the Lender having drawn up papers as if Mr. Lopresti and his Conservator would be the Parties entering into the loan agreement when the position is that the property is in the trust and the Conservatee's future care needs. The Petition for Authority to Borrow Funds for a Reverse Mortgage Loan, and the Petition for Conservator's Authority to Sign Reverse Mortgage Loan are continued to June 23, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for future briefing and for notice to the SPCA.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Dec-18-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Melanie Emmons for David Levin.

The Court noted that when a trust is under Court supervision the Trustee cannot take any fees without a Court order. Ms. Emmons stated that there were so many things going on, that the good thing is that Mr. Lopresti got to stay at home because of Mr. Levin's efforts, and that the billing department in her office and Mr. Levin's billing department didn't coordinate. With respect to the conservatorship fees, Ms. Emmons stated that she takes responsibility for those fees, that she was just starting out as an Attorney and that she has a petition on file asking that the conservatorship fees be paid by the trust. The First Account and Report of Trustee, etc. is under submission.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

MINI MINUTES:

The Court noted that an accounting has not been filed and that the order of February 28, 2018 required an accounting for the period of February 15, 2018 through February 14, 2019. Mr. Levin stated that he was not aware of today's hearing date. The Court noted that Mr. Kato was not present and stated that the fact that Mr. Levin was not aware of the hearing suggest that there is a breakdown in communication. Status on filing of the first accounting is continued to May 22, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Parties are to express the Court's concern to Mr. Kato.

Petition For Authority To Borrow Funds For A Reverse Mortgage Loan
Off Calendar Dec-15-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Brent Kato for and with David Levin (Petitioner).
Amy Harrington Court appointed Counsel for Carl LoPresti.

Mr. Kato provided a proposed order. The Court stated that now that they have Mr. Levin serving as the Conservator and the Trustee, it will require that the Trust be brought under Court supervision and that a bond will be required. The Court also questions how the loan is called (reverse mortgage or negative amortization). The Petition for Authority to Borrow Funds for a Reverse Mortgage is taken under submission. Status on filing of a trust petition is set for January 24, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Kato expressed urgency stating that funds for support of the Conservatee are diminishing.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

Petition For Authority To Borrow Funds For A Reverse Mortgage Loan
Off Calendar Dec-12-2017 Continued To Dec-13-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Ex Parte Petition To Advance Hearing Date Regarding Petition For Authority To Borrow Funds For A Reverse Mortgage Loan
Off Calendar Nov-28-2017 Granted Per Written Order
